ocr_text:
Schenley Chief Giving Away $50- Million; ROSENSTIEL PLANS $50-MILLION GIFTS
Will donate $50-million to educ and charitable insts in next few mos; some of insts that will receive $1-million gifts listed

STUDY GIVES G.O.P. NET GAIN OF 3 TO 7 IN GOVERNORSHIPS; Survey of Campaigns Finds Party Could Hold Office in 29 or in 33 States G.O.P. MAY SCORE IN GOVERNORSHIPS
survey of Gov races

Liquor Concern Will Shift Its Officers After Oct. 1; Schenley Is Planning to Shift Its Top Management
I A Becker expected to be named chmn and B Goldberg pres following election of new bd of dire, Oct 1; chmn Rosenstiel will retire and pres Mackie will undertake special assignments in internatl div; Glen Alden now owns 88% of co

After 5 Months of Paris Talks, U.S. Negotiators Keep Hoping
leaders of Amer delegation still believe war will be settled in talks despite wearying lack of progress after 5 mos; believe that N Vietnamese really do think it is in their interest to stop fighting; wait for some sign of what N Vietnam would be willing to do to achieve end of fighting; are determined to go on with talks; difficulties in case Nixon is elected Pres discussed

SCHOOL REFORMS APPROVED IN PARIS; Cabinet Votes Bill to Give Autonomy to Universities
Cabinet approves Faure's program; details

Chicago Police Action Becomes Connecticut Issue; Senator Ribicoffs Speech Assailing 'Gestapo Tactics' Is Debated Widely
role of Daley and police in Chicago disorders is principal issue in Conn campaign for Sen because of strong pub stand taken by Ribicoff; both Ribicoff and his opponent E H May Jr claim substantial gains from Ribicoff statment at conv

DEMOCRATS SPLIT ON PARTY'S TICKET; Dispute in Missouri Termed Hint of Wider Trouble
Many Dem candidates reptd embarrassed by poor showing Humphrey ticket is making and are trying to run as independently of it as possible

O'DWYER IS TOLD HE 'LETS NIXON IN'; G.O.P. Victory Is Feared in His Snub of Humphrey
O'Dwyer discusses stand on Humphrey with voters; illus; reptdly believes inclusion of McCarthy's name on NYS ballot will help him by drawing supporters to polls

Government Courses Offered to Negro Nominees
Scholarship, Educ and Defense Fund for Racial Equality to sponsor program of crash courses and professional advice for 300 Negro candidates for pub office throughout country who appear to have good chance for election but who have little or no govt experience; program to cost estimated $280,000 1st yr

DOAR TO BE HEAD OF SCHOOL BOARD; Local-Control Advocate Will Be Elected Tomorrow -- Galamison in No. 2 Post Doar Will Head School Board; Galamison to Be Vice President
bd, in informal session, elects J M Doar bd pres, Rev M A Galamison vp; neither nominee gains unanimous approval; present bd vp Brown withdraws from race for re-election and backs Galamison; bd also tentatively approves creation of standing coms for decentralization and ways to renovate school system

Cardin Label Going On U.S.-Made Suits
Cardin licenses US co to mfr men's suits, will continue to sell higher-priced suits from own workrooms in Bonwit Teller boutique, NYC
Names for Nixon Planes

Daley Rejects Offer by N.B.C.; C.B.S. Turns Down His Request
Daley rejects NBC offer to appear on panel TV show and give his version of events that produced chaos in Chicago; Daley aide says Daley wants to use time as he sees fit, unhindered by questions from reporters; CBS turns down Daley's request; ABC spokesman says request has not been received; Chicago Police Dept repts 56 complaints filed for mistreatment by policemen, 22 of them by newsmen and 9 by dept itself; complaints are being revd; Metromedia TV offers Daley 1 hr of prime time; reply awaited

In The Nation: Humphrey's Three Assets
T Wicker comment on outlook for Humphrey holds that he has 3 unusual assets working for him: Nixon, Wallace and Johnson
King Emperor 3-5 Choice in $385,350 Arlington-Washington Futurity Today; 18 SLATED TO GO IN CHICAGO DASH King Emperor Has No. 6 Post for Richest Event in Thoroughbred Racing
Hines Takes 100-Meter Dash Final in 10 Seconds at Olympic Track Trials; MATSON'S 67-1 1/4 IS 3D IN SHOT-PUT Woods Sets Pace at 68-1/4 -- Ryun and Farrell Gain Final in 800-Meter Run
Dr. Fager 1-2 Choice in $100,000 United Nations Handicap in Jersey Today; COLT IS MAKING HIS GRASS DEBUT Will Carry Top Weight of 134 Pounds Against 10 Rivals at Atlantic City

Advertising: Something Out of the Stars?
Tatham & Weihs formed as direct mail response div; F A Weihs gen mgr

Small-Town Iowa Is Worried Over Crime
article on views on campaign of voters of Webster City, Iowa, called typical Iowa town; illus

Advertising: Politics Out of Series Line-Up
Formed by B Young and H Dale

Politics: Wallace Finds Attack on Press and TV Is a Successful Campaign Tactic; AUDIENCES CHEER SCORNFUL VIEWS Response Believed Greater Since Chicago Disorders
G C Wallace attack on 'liberal, left-wing press' said to gain in effectiveness since dispute over police actions in Chicago

Wallace and Nixon Vie in South; Conservatives Shun Humphrey; Ex-Alabama Governor Thought to Lead in 9 of 11 States of Old Confederacy, With Total of 91 Electoral Votes
ints with pol leaders and ind observers in South show Wallace and Nixon in close competition for conservative vote; show Wallace leading in all but 2 of 11 Southern states; show Humphrey chances of eking out plurality all but non-existent except in a few states

Miss Wade Upsets Mrs. King, 6-4, 6-2, and Wins U.S. Open Tennis Title; OKKER AND ASHE ADVANCE TO FINAL Dutch Star Earns $14,000 by Defeating Rosewall -- Graebner Eliminated
US open champs; V Wade (GB) wins women's singles

The Battle Of Chicago: From the Delegates' Side; DELEGATES' SIDE -"At this convention the old-time humor of it all soon began to sour..."
article on Dem Natl Conv from delegates' viewpoint; illus

Six Lean to Humphrey, With Six Indefinite in Times Survey; Study Finds Nixon Is Leading in 30 States and Wallace in Eight
survey of Pres election campaign outlook in all states

5 Candidates Nominated For Posts on Civil Court
NY County Dems nominate L Grossman, M Evans, C Scott, E Goodell, W Mertens; Repub, Liberal endorsements expected

Topics: One Poet's Pitch for the Cardinals to Win the Series
M Moore predicts St Louis victory

Congress to Get Three More Negroes
W L Clay (Dem) and C C Crawford (Repub), both Negroes, seek HR seat, 1st Dist

AIRLINES TO MEET ON NEW JETPORT; Solberg Expected to Win Support on Thursday
15 US and foreign lines serving area will offer Gov Hughes their proposals for 4th jetport site in NJ
U.S. Expected to Win the Most Medals (112) and the Most Gold Medals (43); Major Hopes In Swimming And in Track

Need for Electoral Reform
on need for Electoral Coll reform
CASPER FAVORED TO WIN ALCAN GOLF; Bad Weather May Put Off Start of Matches Today

Republican Efforts to Gain Support Among Negroes Appear to Have Failed
Article on Repub party's failure to gain support among Negroes
Tarkenton's Return to Georgia Expected to Be a Giant Success
Harvard's Shaggies Disappointing in Rowing Trial
U.S. Equestrian Team Favored To Win Gold Medal in Mexico

EFFORT BY DALEY FOR TICKET LAGS; Humphrey's Rally in Chicago Falls Short of Johnson's
Humphrey comments on turmoil in Chicago

Will the Blacks Say, 'Too Little, Too Late'?
Article on role of and portrayal of Negroes in forthcoming TV season; illus

One-Way Containers Make Further Gains
Can Mfrs Inst repts beer can production rose to 12.9-billion in '66, compared to 8.7-billion in '61; estimates '67 and '68 figures will be much higher

Radicals, Relaxing on Illinois Farm, Relieve Chicago
about 300 youths protesting war and police action in Chicago demonstrate across from White House and in front of Humphrey hq in Washington

Brasilia Is Growing Rapidly
Govt presses construction in Brasilia in effort to create major city in interior

LAGOS SUGGESTS NEUTRAL AIRSTRIP; Gowan Gives Plan for Aid to Biafrans in TV Talk
Gowon says Nigeria would agree to have relief supplies flown to airstrip now used for mercy and arms shipments if strip is 'neutralized,' radio and TV s; says victory is in sight, scores 'evil influence of foreign meddlers,' warns of foreign conspiracy against Nigeria; Fed irritation over internatl diplomatic pressure for mercy airlift noted
Health Institutes' Era of Expansion Ends as Leadership Changes

Computer Industry Short of Personnel
Article on growing shortage of qualified personnel in computer indus; to overcome shortage, users of equipment have been 'stealing' trained personnel of competitors by offering them higher salaries and larger fringe benefits, as well as flooding news media with ads; growth of indus discussed; expanding interest in time-sharing noted
Nomination of Humphrey: Details of a 5-Month Drive; Campaign Started With Withdrawal of Johnson and Ended With a Phone Call From Kennedy Just Before Voting

Law; Crime Figures Show Something New
comment on FBI statistics showing crime is rising at a dangerous rate; chart

Sales Hopes for 1969 Auto Models Outshine the Chrome; Sales Hopes for 1969 Cars Are Bright
speculation on '69 outlook; many execs see continuation of '68 boom
